channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Second Australian teen dies in tainted alcohol case in Laos that has killed 6 tourists
item.description: VANG VIENG, Laos (AP) — A second Australian teenager who fell critically ill after drinking tainted alcohol in Laos has died in a hospital in Bangkok, her family said Friday, bringing the death toll in the mass poisoning of foreign tourists to six.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Murdered Kansas mom suffered 30 stab, cut wounds trying to defend herself: autopsy
item.description: An Oklahoma medical examiner's office has released the full autopsy report for the one of the two Kansas moms found buried in a freezer in a cow pasture earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canoeist paddling 6,000-mile Great Loop out of gratitude for life
item.description: ANNAPOLIS, Md. (AP) — Peter Frank has paddled from Michigan's Upper Peninsula in June to the Chesapeake Bay in Maryland this month in his 1982 Sawyer Loon decked canoe, but he’s still got a long way to go.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Texas is taking a final vote on allowing Bible-infused lessons in public schools
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas would allow Bible-infused lessons in elementary schools under changes that were set for a final vote Friday and could test boundaries between religion and public education in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Diddy private eyes confront influencers about possible payments
item.description: TikTokers Samson Crouppen and Amala Ekpunobi deny any pay arrangement and expressed fear and concern over the visits.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about a Wisconsin man who faked his own death and fled to Eastern Europe
item.description: A sheriff says a Wisconsin man who faked his own drowning this summer and abandoned his wife and three children is in Eastern Europe.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: JD Vance is leaving the Senate for the vice presidency. That's set off a scramble for his Ohio seat
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — JD Vance's election as vice president has opened up one of Ohio's U.S. Senate seats for the third time in as many years, setting off a scramble for the appointment among the state's ruling Republicans. GOP Gov.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Catherine Herridge wants Trump 'sit-down' on bipartisan press bill
item.description: “This case involves the First Amendment and the right of reporters in the future,” Catherine Herridge said.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Trump has promised again to release the last JFK files. But experts say don’t expect big revelations
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — More than 60 years after President John F. Kennedy was assassinated, conspiracy theories still swirl and any new glimpse into the fateful day of Nov. 22, 1963, in Dallas continues to fascinate.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Alabama carries out nation’s third nitrogen gas execution on a man for a hitchhiker's killing
item.description: ATMORE, Ala. (AP) — An Alabama man convicted in the 1994 killing of a hitchhiker cursed at the prison warden and made obscene gestures with his hands shortly before he was put to death Thursday evening in the nation’s third execution using nitrogen gas.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: North Korean leader says past diplomacy only confirmed US hostility
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un said his past negotiations with the United States only confirmed Washington’s “unchangeable” hostility toward his country and described his nuclear buildup as the only way to counter external threats, state media said Friday.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Alabama carries out nitrogen gas execution on inmate convicted in female hitchhiker's 1994 killing
item.description: Carey Dale Grayson, a 50-year-old Alabama inmate convicted in the 1994 murder of a female hitchhiker, was put to death on Thursday using nitrogen gas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NYC stabbing spree shows failures of criminal justice system: Mayor
item.description: “It’s a public health safety issue,” Adams said regarding the triple homicide at a news conference Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Passengers duct tape unruly man who tried to open door on American Airlines plane
item.description: A group of passengers aboard a Dallas-bound flight restrained a man with duct tape after he claimed to be the "captain" and attempted to open the cabin door of an American Airlines plane midair, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers make last-ditch effort to safeguard US support
item.description: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers in both parties are working to get key provisions surrounding U.S. support for Kyiv passed into law by January, an effort to safeguard assistance that is under threat in a transition to a Trump administration and full GOP control of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5

item.title: Trump chooses Pam Bondi for attorney general pick after Gaetz withdraws
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Thursday named Pam Bondi, the former attorney general of Florida, to be U.S. attorney general just hours after his other choice, Matt Gaetz, withdrew his name.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: South Dakota Republican introduces bill to eliminate Department of Education
item.description: The proposal would get rid of the federal agency and move all critical programs to other departments.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats will push for release of Gaetz report despite withdrawal from AG running
item.description: Rep. Sean Casten (D-Ill.), who had introduced a resolution on releasing the report, said the public still has the right to know.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: There are massive Black Friday deals on coveted North Face gear
item.description: Whether you need a warm winter coat for your kids, a snowsuit for anyone in your family or some sleeping bags and shoes — this sale has you covered.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Carte, NewsNation

item.title: Walmart cinnamon rolls, danish recalled over possible plastic contamination
item.description: "A sales restriction was placed on impacted products, and they were removed from impacted stores," the statement said.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Self, NewsNation

item.title: Gaetz withdrawal from AG nomination is among quickest in history
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) withdrew his nomination as President-elect Trump’s attorney general on Thursday, putting an end to a chaotic several days on Capitol Hill and making him one of the quickest in history to withdraw from Cabinet nominations.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Historically bad year for dolphin strandings on Cape Cod has scientists looking for answers
item.description: An unprecedentedly bad year for beached dolphins on Cape Cod might have to do with warming waters changing the availability of the animals' food, said scientists hoping to curb the strandings.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 73% worry Social Security won't pay promised benefits: Survey
item.description: Most nonretired U.S. adults, 73%, are worried they won't get their promised Social Security benefits when they retire.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Police report reveals assault allegations against Hegseth, Trump's pick for defense secretary
item.description: SANTA CRUZ, Calif. (AP) — A woman told police that she was sexually assaulted in 2017 by Pete Hegseth after he took her phone, blocked the door to a California hotel room and refused to let her leave, according to a detailed investigative report made public late Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA MENDOZA, BRIAN SLODYSKO and JULIET LINDERMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What to know about a storm bringing high winds, heavy rain, snow to California and Pacific Northwest
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — One of the strongest storms on the West Coast in decades knocked out power for thousands of people, unleashed strong winds that toppled trees and left two dead in Washington before making its way through Oregon to Northern California where on Thursday it dropped heavy snow and r
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Missing father of 3 did 'research' to stage his own death and flee the country: sheriff
item.description: Wisconsin officials have successfully contacted Ryan Borgwardt, a missing father of three who "staged his death" in August and apparently fled the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Carolina official overseeing hurricane rebuilding efforts is no longer in role
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — The top official for North Carolina's efforts to rebuild communities after natural disasters is no longer working in the position just a few days after a scathing rebuke of her work from GOP state lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fugitive arrested after Chicago standoff blocks away from DNC is being returned to Mississippi
item.description: At the time of the escape, the fugitive was set to make a court appearance on Mississippi charges of attempted murder and armed robbery.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Most say they probably won't get updated COVID vaccine: Survey
item.description: It has been over four years since the U.S. began dealing with COVID-19, with the spread of the virus causing mass shutdowns within multiple industries and people around the world being forced to stay in their homes.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: Las Vegas man facing eviction tried to kill puppies, police say
item.description: A man allegedly tried to kill several puppies by covering them in cleaning products and placing them in a trash bag in a dumpster because he was getting evicted, according to police documents.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, Fox 5

item.title: Best apps to save and organize your recipes
item.description: How many times have you found a great recipe online only to wonder, "Now, where do I save this?" TikTok and Instagram have built-in bookmark functionality, but these apps not only help you save and organize your collection but also cut through the clutter. Mela, Free to $5, iOS and Mac only.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Italian village offering $1 homes to Americans wanting to relocate after election
item.description: A small rural village in Italy is offering homes for as little as $1.06 after the 2024 presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, ABC 4

item.title: CMA Awards 2024: Everything to know about Country Music's Biggest Night
item.description: Get ready for the "biggest night" in country music with everything you need to know for the 58th Annual CMA Awards.
item.dc:creator: Sebastian Posey, KRON4

item.title: Texas board advances plan to allow Bible material in elementary school lessons
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas' education board on Tuesday advanced a new Bible-infused curriculum that would be optional for schools to incorporate in kindergarten through fifth grades, one of the latest Republican-led efforts in the U.S. to incorporate more religious teaching into classrooms.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Reuters hires former Post and AP executive editor Sally Buzbee to lead US, Canada news operation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Sally Buzbee, the former executive editor of The Washington Post and The Associated Press, is joining Reuters as the news agency's top editor for the United States and Canada, the company said Tuesday. As news editor for the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The worst passwords you could have in 2024 — or any year
item.description: Don't rely on easy-to-remember passwords too much — because all of us tend to think more alike than we'd think.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, WGN 9
